title: Classes, Modifiers, Private State, and Abstract Contracts · Compiler Case: Abstract Implements
summary: Implement scheduling components with runtime invariants, replaceable policies, and explicit construction rules. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts abstract implements increment in a typed scheduling component library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use abstract classes and implements clauses while recognizing that implementation checks do not change inferred member types.
Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
